title: Book of Common Prayer (1928) -- Collects
tradition_notes: The 1928 Book of Common Prayer served the Episcopal Church (USA) from 1928 until 1979. Its collects draw on older pre-Reformation sources, Thomas Cranmer's 1549/1552/1662 BCPs, and revisions adopted by the General Convention. This dataset covers the Sunday, Feast Day, and special occasion collects from the Collects, Epistles, and Gospels section.
source_edition: episcopalnet.org digitization of the Book of Common Prayer (1928, USA)
notes: The 1928 BCP is unambiguously in the public domain (published 1928, all authors long deceased). The source site (episcopalnet.org) has no robots.txt. A 2-second courtesy crawl delay is used between page requests. 100 individual HTML pages cover the full propers (Collects, Epistles, and Gospels) for all Sundays, Holy Days, and special occasions. Good Friday has three collects on a single page; these are extracted as separate prayer records. Exception: the Trinity23 and Trinity24 pages on episcopalnet.org contain hymn suggestions only -- no collect text. The collects for these two Sundays were sourced from the 1928 US BCP scan on Internet Archive (https://archive.org/details/1928bookofcommon0000prot, pages 222-223, fetched 2026-04-01). Note: the 1928 US BCP text differs from the 1662 CoE BCP on these two collects (Trinity23 uses a semicolon after "godliness"; Trinity24 uses "for the sake of Jesus Christ" rather than "for Jesus Christ's sake").
